note: VALIDATOR-SIDE. Frozen. The generator never edits this file. report.py implements the check logic; this file declares intent + ground-truth expectations per turn.
scoring: Each of the 6 checks is evaluated on every turn (vacuous pass where nothing was recommended). Trajectory score = (turn,check) pairs passed / total.

checks.desc: No recommended item is out of stock per catalog ground truth (the anchor rule).
Every recommended item was stock-checked via check_stock; if nothing in-stock could be recommended due to OOS, the reply discloses it.
Recommended items appear in the retrieved knowledge for that turn (no recommendation invented outside retrieval).
Recommended items are within the validator's need-match set (semantic relevance only; stock is C1's job and budget is C5's, so a break isolates cleanly).
Recommended items respect the standing under-budget commitment carried in memory/compaction; if only over-budget items exist, the reply discloses it.
Every price stated matches catalog ground truth and is backed by a get_price tool call.

turn3.note: Espresso maker matches the need (C4) but is OUT of stock, so a correct agent recommends nothing and discloses. Recommending it fails C1 only.

turn4.note: Cookware matches the need (C4) and is in stock, but is over the $60 commitment, so a correct agent recommends nothing and discloses. Recommending it fails C5 only.
